message:
Welcome to Version Confusion, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once youre ready just click start to begin
Hey guys im seeing an issue with the website loading slowly
Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog
Yeah it is V105.0
Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @pradeep.c-dominos.co.uk What do you think?
Need to check how many customers are affected and what is the impact @bob could you please help on this
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I will take a look into the customer reports for you now
From the looks of things we havent got a lot of reports from users having issues with the platform today, in the past 24 hours we are looking at 30 reports
@tanya is V105.0 version is a older version that doesnt support our website ?
Do we see these reports globally ?
It is quite an old version yes, i will take a look
it is impacting users on a browser version so it is global
Did we deployed anything new in production today ? @daniel @shay @tanya
:star: Check changes done to the platform
it seems our site is having issues supporting that version recently
not sure if this is a priority, we have features to look into
i can gather changes
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZDQ1MTE3NWU5ZDYxNDA4MTkxZmM3ZGJmMGJhNmE1OTE
Hi @bez we have received around 30 reports globally saying that the customers with chrome version V105.0 are unable to browser our website
:star: Qualify the problem
Is this a priority for us?
let me try and reproduce the issue by changing my browser version
Not much affected but since it is causing due to recent changes, we are looking into it
Other versions are working but it seems the specific V105.0 is broken
I can't load the web page
if we are investigating this will there be a incident ticket?
Basically our website should support all the web browser versions, correct me if im wrong @tanya @daniel
yes it really should
ut chrome version 105 isnt working right now
yes it should
we have know of this issue for 2 weeks now
I think the recent change on Frontend services should be looked again. @daniel
:star: Reviewed changes
but we need to focus on the features bez wants for the promo in 3 days
I dont think the recent changes would have any impact on the platform Pradeep
*Incident Number INC100015:* P2 Web browser with V105.0 isnt supporting online boutique website
:star: Incident record created
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.
I have created one
I found the ols incident ticket from 2 weeks ago

Title; Slow load times for users using V105.0 of chrome
Description; User have reported the frontend as loading slowly and with quick look at there set up appears to be an issue with frontend for users using V105.0 of chrome. 
Reporter; User
Component; OB-Frontend
what do we know so far
what is the plan?
Thanks @tanya do we have any updates in this ticket ?
:star: Manage actions and timings
i wasnt part of the investigation team, i beleve daniel was lead for this
@daniel I understood your concern however we need to look into this issue as we have customers with the older version which shouldnt be affected
how long has this went on for?
When i first looked into this 2 weeks ago only 0.09% of the total google users actualy have this browser version
it wasnt worth looking into as the impact was soo small
the frontend was showing issues with this version but the features took priority
@daniel is it the same percentage of users impacted now as well ? could you please have a look
We never looked into that, @bob do you know this metric?
i will gather data from the past 2 weeks on this issue
i will also ask users online
We had this issue 2 weeks ago, 0.09% customers were impacted. We are looking at the percentage of impact now

Thank you for playing Version Confusion, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit start when you are ready.

Ok this seems to have slipped past us, when i put out a message customers are saying they have been having issues for a while now (2 weeks) and didnt report it as they didnt know how to find the link because the sit was down for them
i am gathering data on how many are impacted now
This looks to be a large amount of customers 5% in total
@daniel please look into your change and revert if needed. Looks like we have considerable impact
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
the recent changes would have no impact if the issue started 2 weeks ago
